title: Eryngium amathymbicum
intro: Eryngium amathymbicum, also known as Wolf's Bane or Eryngo, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. Traditionally, Eryngium amathymbicum has been used for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Eryngium amathymbicum is commonly used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments, although its efficacy and safety have not been extensively scientifically proven.
taxonomy: Eryngium amathymbicum belongs to the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. This family consists of plants with flowers that are typically small and white or yellow in color, arranged in an umbrella-like shape. Eryngium amathymbicum is further classified under the genus Eryngium, a group of flowering plants often referred to as sea hulles. The species Eryngium amathymbicum is native to the southwestern region of North America. It is a perennial plant that is often found growing in dry, sandy areas.
